pi_items.verified_value: TransID (appropriate title for dns.id Transaction ID field)

pi_items.verified_value: Query (appropriate title for dns.qry.name Query Name field)

Wireshark packet list documentation (https://www.wireshark.org/docs/wsug_html_chunked/ChUsePacketListPaneSection.html) - 'Length' is confirmed as one of the seven default columns in Wireshark's standard packet list pane

Wireshark Display Filter Reference (https://www.wireshark.org/docs/dfref/d/dns.html) - verified as valid DNS filter expression using 'dns.flags.rcode' field ('Reply code', Unsigned integer, versions 1.0.0 to 4.6.4). Tested against official Wireshark dns.cap sample file: filter matches 6 DNS error packets with NXDOMAIN responses (rcode=3)
Path syntax verification - valid absolute path to user's Documents directory

privileged_info_summary: The task uses official Wireshark sample data (dns.cap) from the public Wireshark project (https://wiki.wireshark.org). The DNS dissector field names are verified: dns.id is the Transaction ID field, dns.qry.name is the Query Name field, and dns.flags.rcode is the Reply Code field. The filter expression 'dns.flags.rcode != 0' is valid and matches DNS error responses in the sample capture file. The 'Length' column is a confirmed standard Wireshark packet list column. The sample dns.cap file contains diverse DNS traffic including queries for google.com, www.netbsd.org, www.example.com, and reverse DNS lookups, with at least 6 NXDOMAIN error responses (rcode=3) that would be highlighted by the coloring rule.
